sglang.title: Filter empty prompt in random bench serving
sglang.author: ispobock
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Fix issue:
 RuntimeWarning: divide by zero encountered in scalar floor_divide
  ratio = (input_lens[i] + prompt_len - 1) // prompt_len

Reproduce:
python3 -m sglang.bench_serving --backend sglang --dataset-path ShareGPT_V3_unfiltered_cleaned_split.json --dataset-name random --random-input

sglang.title: support echo=true and logprobs in openai api when logprobs=1 in lm-evaluation-harness
sglang.author: BBuf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Sglang service can't match lm-evaluation-harness, because lm-evaluation-harness set echo=True and logprobs=1 in openai client api: https://github.com/EleutherAI/lm-evaluation-harness/blob/bd80a6c0099ee207e70f4943117739a817eccc0b/lm_eval/models/openai_completions.py#L55
This pr solved it, I can run l

vllm.title: [V1] Allow `tokenizer_mode` and `trust_remote_code` for Detokenizer
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR properly passes tokenizer_mode and trust_remote_code from ModelConfig to the Detokenizer process. This was previously missed and would cause the engine to be stuck when testing with certain models that requires remote code for its detokenizer. (e.g, InternVL)
Perhaps it's also a good idea to

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Hardware][CPU] Fix broken encoder-decoder CPU runner
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix broken encoder-decoder CPU runner due to #10193
I forgot implementing sampling preparation when refactoring CPU encoder-decoder runner😅

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Bumps actions/setup-python from 5.2.0 to 5.3.0.

Release notes
Sourced from actions/setup-python's releases.

v5.3.0
What's Changed

Add workflow file for publishing releases to immutable action package by @​Jcambass in actions/setup-python#941
Upgrade IA publish by @​Jcambass in actions/setup-pytho

vllm.title: [V1] Fix detokenizer ports
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR fixes the port mismatch introduced in #10211

vllm.title: [V1] Do not use inductor for piecewise CUDA graphs
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR disables the use of TorchInductor, to avoid the compilation time and issue. We can flip the flag again once the compiler becomes more stable.

vllm.title: [v1][torch.compile] support managing cudagraph buffer
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
advantage: caller does not need to explicitly manage the input buffer
disadvantage: the cost of one more input tensor, and one more copy kernel.
given the code simplification from user side, i think it should be worthwhile.

vllm.title: [V1] Use custom ops for piecewise CUDA graphs
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: A change omitted from #10225

vllm.title: Add docs on serving with Llama Stack

vllm.title: [misc][distributed] auto port selection and disable tests
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: observed flakiness in ci, e.g. https://buildkite.com/vllm/fastcheck/builds/7714#01931945-8b3f-4a69-a9fe-4f933c0a44e2 and https://buildkite.com/vllm/ci-aws/builds/11061#01931a04-7249-4e65-881c-94deb12c98c6 . might be caused by port conflict. use auto selection instead.

vllm.title: [V1] Enable custom ops with piecewise CUDA graphs

vllm.title: [Doc] Update help text for `--distributed-executor-backend`
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The help text for --distributed-executor-backend indicated that the
option would deafult to ray any time multiple GPUs are used. This
did not reflect the current logic for choosing a default. When
multiple GPUs can be used on a single host, it will default to mp
instead of ray, except in the case of

vllm.title: [1/N] torch.compile user interface design
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
previously, we need to set the env var before importing the model. that's quite confusing.
now we only read env var when we initialize the model.
the next step is to move them to a compilation config, bundled with the model's vllm_config

vllm.title: [Misc][LoRA] Replace hardcoded cuda device with configurable argument
vllm.author: jeejeelee
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #9794  (link existing issues this PR will resolve)
This PR replaces the hardcoded CUDA device with a configurable argument, which not only makes the code more flexible but also paves the way for future LoRA support on other hardware devices.

vllm.title: Splitting attention kernel file
vllm.author: maleksan85
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Split paged attention kernel into two files for v1 and v2 to speed up compilation when template instantiation explodes.
side effect: support Navi32
